context.read: TileLang grouped-D direct-source did not become viable when D-block reuse was increased, so the sparse-dKV ladder returns to the HIP/rocWMMA route-grid surface.

baseline_correction.issue: Two prior actual-LI rocWMMA pair-slot artifacts showed raw_atomic around 46 ms versus 10 ms for the same nominal S4096/T32/K512 shape.
baseline_correction.root_cause: The 46 ms artifact used invalid selected slots encoded as group 0. The raw_atomic kernel still issued zero-valued atomics into group 0 for invalid slots. The default invalid sentinel is -1, which skips invalid slots and is the meaningful raw baseline.

why: The clean raw atomic rocWMMA baseline is about 10.06 ms, not the invalid-group-zero 46 ms debug number.
Pair-slot metadata is correct and compact, and it models 23.8x fewer global writes.
The current pair-slot kernel still takes 18.53 ms because it performs one local table update per selected contribution plus dense active-slot initialization/emission.
The selected-row dKV write delta in clean raw_atomic is only about 1.11 ms over contrib_sink, so reducing global atomics alone cannot pay for a heavy local reducer.
keep: Actual-LI pair_slots or equivalent compact route metadata.
The clean invalid_group_value=-1 contract for raw and compact comparisons.
reject: Using invalid_group_zero raw timings as a baseline.
Dense active_group x D local tables as the production reducer.
More shape sweeps of compact_pairslot_atomic unless the reducer topology changes.
next_kernel_contract: Keep the query-major MFMA contribution tile.
Do not write selected-row partials to global memory.
Do not accumulate every selected contribution into a dense active-group LDS table.
Use warp/lane/block segmented reduction over sparse pair slots, or a CK/rocPRIM-style segmented reducer, so only touched-group segments are initialized and reduced.
Beat the clean raw_atomic 10.06 ms and the retained Triton sparse-atomic wall around 8.0-8.8 ms before any TorchTitan e2e promotion.
